key_themes: Strong Q3 FY2026 earnings beat with highest revenue growth in 10 quarters
Diabetes (MiniMed) spin-off and related charges leading to lowered FY2026 EPS guidance
Moderate Buy analyst consensus with ~25% upside to average price targets of $108-109
Strategic transformation, activist involvement (Elliott), and innovation in cardiovascular, neuroscience, and PFA
Attractive valuation amid core business momentum but cautious retail views on historical underperformance
summary: Medtronic reported strong Q3 FY2026 results with $9.0B revenue (8.7% growth) and EPS of $1.36, beating estimates and marking the best organic growth in years. A subsequent cut to full-year EPS guidance due to diabetes business charges and spin-off plans created near-term pressure, with the stock trading near $86. Analysts largely view it positively as a value opportunity, maintaining Moderate Buy ratings with significant upside, supported by activist input, product innovation, and a sharper post-spin-off focus on higher-growth segments.
sources: 15+ news articles on Q3 earnings beat (Feb 2026), guidance cut (Mar 2026), and upcoming Q4 (Jun 2026), 20+ analyst ratings with recent PT adjustments (mostly Buy/Outperform, targets $91-121), Multiple Reddit threads (r/ValueInvesting, r/dividends, r/stocks, r/MedicalDevices), Limited relevant X posts (~10 retrieved, low engagement on equity-specific sentiment)
